name: Jägerbomb
instructions: Pour a cold can of energy drink into a pint glass. Drop the full shot glass of Jägermeister straight in, glass and all, and drink immediately while it fizzes.

name: Soju Bomb (Somaek)
instructions: Fill a pint glass halfway with very cold lager. Drop in a shot glass of soju and drink while it foams. Korea's somaek is the original soju bomb: the name mashes soju and maekju, the Korean word for beer.

name: Sake Bomb
instructions: Balance a shot of warm sake on chopsticks laid across a half-full glass of cold lager. Slam the table so the shot drops in, then drink straight down.

name: Pickleback
instructions: Pour two shots side by side: one of whiskey, one of cold pickle brine. Knock back the whiskey, then chase it immediately with the brine. The salt and vinegar erase the burn.

name: Irish Slammer
instructions: Pour half a glass of stout and let it settle. In a shot glass, layer Irish cream over Irish whiskey. Drop the shot into the stout and drink straight down before it curdles.

name: Hugo Spritz
instructions: Fill a large wine glass with ice. Add the elderflower liqueur, gently squeeze in the lime, then top with prosecco and a splash of soda. Stir once and slap the mint before dropping it in.

name: Negroni Sbagliato
instructions: Build over ice: sweet vermouth, then Campari, then top with prosecco. Stir once and garnish with an orange slice. Sbagliato means 'mistaken': a Milanese bartender reached for sparkling wine instead of gin and kept it.

name: Ranch Water
instructions: Fill a highball with ice, add the tequila and lime juice, then top with very cold sparkling mineral water. West Texas tradition says to drink some straight from the bottle and mix the rest in it.

name: Paper Plane
instructions: Shake equal parts bourbon, Aperol, amaro and lemon juice hard with ice. Double strain into a chilled coupe. A modern classic from 2008, named after the M.I.A. song playing when it was invented.

name: U-Boot
instructions: Fill a pint glass most of the way with cold lager. Sink a full shot glass of vodka to the bottom like a submarine and drink. A German and Polish party standard.

name: Shikanji (Nimbu Pani)
instructions: Stir the lemon juice, sugar, black salt and roasted cumin into cold water until dissolved. Pour over ice, top with a splash of soda and slap a mint sprig over the glass. North India's roadside lemonade, sold from carts every summer.

name: Jal-Jeera
instructions: Blend the mint with cumin, black salt, tamarind and lemon juice, then stir into cold water and strain over ice. Sharp, smoky and savoury: the name means cumin water, and it is drunk across India as both cooler and appetiser.

name: Aam Panna
instructions: Roast or boil a raw green mango until soft, scoop the pulp and blend with sugar, roasted cumin, black salt and mint. Dilute with cold water over ice. The tart green-mango cooler that gets North India through the loo winds of May.

name: Masala Chaas
instructions: Whisk the yogurt with cold water until thin and frothy, then stir in roasted cumin, black salt, grated ginger and chopped mint or coriander. Served after lunch across India: the spiced buttermilk that cools better than anything fizzy.

name: Kokum Cooler
instructions: Stir the kokum syrup with lime juice and a pinch of black salt, pour over ice and top with soda. Deep magenta and pleasantly sour, made from the kokum fruit of the Konkan coast. A measure of white rum or gin slips in easily.

name: Feni Ginger Fizz
instructions: Build over plenty of ice: cashew feni, fresh lime juice and a small pinch of salt, then top with cold ginger ale and stir once. The way Goa's susegad afternoons take their fierce local spirit.

name: Monk & Cola
instructions: Fill a highball with ice, pour the dark rum, top with cold cola and squeeze in a lime wedge. India's default order for generations, built on Old Monk: the vanilla-heavy Ghaziabad rum with a cult following and no advertising.

name: Masala Chai Toddy
instructions: Brew strong masala chai with ginger, cardamom and black pepper. Stir the honey into a warm mug, add the whisky, then pour over the hot chai and finish with a lemon wheel. An Indian winter answer to the hot toddy.

name: Nariyal Mojito
instructions: Muddle the mint gently with sugar and lime juice, add the white rum and tender coconut water, fill with ice and top with a splash of soda. The beach-shack mojito of coastal India, softer and rounder on coconut water.

name: Gondhoraj Gimlet
instructions: Shake the gin hard with gondhoraj lime juice and sugar syrup, double strain into a chilled coupe and express a strip of the fragrant peel over the top. Kolkata's beloved gondhoraj lebu turned this gimlet into a modern Indian bar classic.
